content.problemStatement:
Sushil is a diabetic patient. He is only allowed to eat **at most** KKK calories in a day. However, he likes to eat sweets a lot.

There are NNN sweets in front of him. The iii-th sweet has a calorie count of AiA_iAi​.

Sushil will eat the sweets **in order**, i.e. he will start from the 111-st sweet, then the 222-nd, then the 333-rd, and so on.

If eating the iii-th sweet would take him over his daily calorie limit, he will not eat it, and he will also **not eat any further sweets.**

Find the maximum number of sweets Sushil can eat without exceeding his calorie limit.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of sweets and the calorie limit, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers — A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of sweets Sushil can eat in order without exceeding his calorie limit.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Sushil can eat the first 222 sweets for a total calorie count of 5+2=75+2=75+2=7. If he tries to eat the 333rd sweet, he exceeds his calorie limit of 101010, so he immediately stops.
Test case 333: Sushil is unable to eat the first sweet itself because it has 111111 calories and he can have at most 101010 calories.
